// SLIDE 01 — HOOK

CHAPTER 4 — CLAUDE CODE AS ENGINEERING PARTNER

A developer pastes a broken function into Claude chat. Claude produces a corrected version. The developer copies it back

Chapter 4 — Claude Code as Engineering P — This is the mismatch the chapter is about. Claude chat is the right tool for explaining code, discussing design options,
NARRATION

A developer pastes a broken function into Claude chat. Claude produces a corrected version. The developer copies it back in. Thirty seconds later the tests fail in a new way , because the function was broken for a reason that lived three files away, and Claude chat had no idea those files existed. This is the mismatch the chapter is about. Claude chat is the right tool for explaining code, discussing design options, or sketching a pseudocode plan. It is the wrong tool when the work requires the codebase.

// SLIDE 02 — WHAT THIS CHAPTER LETS YOU DO

WHAT THIS CHAPTER LETS YOU DO.

·Explain the difference between Claude chat for code advice and Claude Code for codebase work
·Decide when a coding task needs repository context and a verification oracle
Describe the engineering workflow loopplan, permission, edit, run, inspect, decide
·Name the verification evidence for a coding task (tests, diffs, builds, lints)
·Understand what permissions control and why they matter
NARRATION

Explain the difference between Claude chat for code advice and Claude Code for codebase work Decide when a coding task needs repository context and a verification oracle Describe the engineering workflow loop: plan, permission, edit, run, inspect, decide Name the verification evidence for a coding task (tests, diffs, builds, lints) Understand what permissions control and why they matter Know which kinds of work Claude Code should not handle alone

// SLIDE 03 — THE CORE CONCEPT: CODE REQUIRE

THE CORE CONCEPT: CODE REQUIRES A VERIFICATION ORACLE

The most important thing to understand about coding agents is not what they can do. It is how correctness gets establish

The Core Concept: Code Requires a Verifi — Code is not done when it is written. It is not done when it reads correctly. It is done when it passes external verifica
NARRATION

The most important thing to understand about coding agents is not what they can do. It is how correctness gets established. Code is not done when it is written. It is not done when it reads correctly. It is done when it passes external verification: tests run, builds complete, type checks pass, linters clear, the diff is reviewed by a human who understands the system. Mark Chen and colleagues (2021) introduced HumanEval, the field's foundational code-evaluation benchmark, precisely because code cannot be judged by appearance.

// SLIDE 04 — WHAT CLAUDE CODE ACTUALLY IS

WHAT CLAUDE CODE ACTUALLY IS.

·Read every file in your project
·Edit files and create new ones
·Run shell commands, builds, and tests
·Inspect test output and iterate
·Show diffs before writing
NARRATION

Claude Code is an agentic coding assistant that runs in the terminal and connects to your development environment (Anthropic, "Claude Code overview"). It is not a separate AI model; it is Claude with tools: file reading, file editing, command execution, test running, web search, and repository-wide context. This is the important difference from chat. Chat has the conversation. Claude Code has the conversation and the codebase. It can:

// SLIDE 05 — THE ENGINEERING PARTNER LOOP

THE ENGINEERING PARTNER LOOP.

Define the issue.→Establish scope.→Review the plan.→Approve permissions.→Inspect the diff.
NARRATION

Using Claude Code well means running a loop, not issuing a single command. Define the issue. Be specific. "Fix the bug in the authentication module" is not a specification. "The login function at auth/login.py:42 throws a KeyError when email is missing from the request body. Fix it so the function returns a 400 response with a clear message instead of crashing. Do not change the response schema for successful logins."

// SLIDE 06 — TASK ROUTING TABLE

TASK ROUTING TABLE.

Explain a code snippet — Good fit
Fix a bug across multiple files — Weak fit
Generate pseudocode or design — Good fit
Run tests and inspect failure — Cannot access
NARRATION

Not every coding-related task needs Claude Code. The routing rule is: if the task requires the codebase, use Claude Code. If the task requires thinking about code, chat is usually enough.

// SLIDE 07 — WORKED WORKFLOW: REPAIRING A F

WORKED WORKFLOW: REPAIRING A FAILING TEST

Situation:→Step 1 — Define the issue:→Step 2 — Review the plan:→Step 3 — Approve the edit:→Step 4 — Run the oracle:
NARRATION

Here is a realistic example of the loop in practice. Situation: A continuous integration build failed overnight. One test in the data-processing module is now throwing a TypeError after a dependency was updated. You have limited time.

// SLIDE 08 — WHAT CLAUDE CODE SHOULD NOT HA

WHAT CLAUDE CODE SHOULD NOT HANDLE ALONE.

·Design decisions. Whether the architecture is right for your system. Whether a refactor is worth the risk.
·Security review. AI-generated code may introduce vulnerabilities; security is a human responsibility with tools and proc
·Secrets management. Credentials, API keys, tokens, and sensitive configuration should not enter Claude Code's context at
·Production deployment decisions. Merging, deploying, and releasing require human accountability.
·Technical debt triage. Whether to fix now or defer, and what the downstream cost is, requires knowing the team, the road
NARRATION

Claude Code is an engineering partner, not an engineering replacement. The following remain human judgment calls: Design decisions. Whether the architecture is right for your system. Whether a refactor is worth the risk. Security review. AI-generated code may introduce vulnerabilities; security is a human responsibility with tools and processes, not a byproduct of clean-looking code. Secrets management. Credentials, API keys, tokens, and sensitive configuration should not enter Claude Code's context at all. Production deployment decisions. Merging, deploying, and releasing require human accountability. Technical debt triage.

// SLIDE 09 — COMMON MISTAKES

COMMON MISTAKES

Treating Claude Code as Claude chat with more power.→Assuming compiled = correct.→Giving Claude Code a vague feature request.→Auto-approving permission prompts.→Skipping the diff.
NARRATION

Treating Claude Code as Claude chat with more power. The difference is environmental access and execution capability , both of which require stronger supervision, not weaker. Assuming compiled = correct. Code that compiles is not verified code. Run the tests. Read the diff.

// SLIDE 10 — BRIDGE TO CHAPTER 5

BRIDGE TO CHAPTER 5.

Claude Code is an agent for software environments. The next chapter introduces an agent for knowledge-work environments:

NARRATION

Claude Code is an agent for software environments. The next chapter introduces an agent for knowledge-work environments: Claude Cowork. Where Claude Code's verification oracle is the test suite, Cowork's verification paths are messier , a spreadsheet that needs row counts, a report that needs source checking, a deck that needs human editorial judgment. The supervision discipline is the same; the tools for checking are different.

// SLIDE 11 — THESIS

THE CORE CONCEPT: CODE REQUIRES A VERIFI

The most important thing to understand about coding agents is not what they can do. It is how correctness gets establish

Code is not done when it is written. It is not done when it reads correctly. It is done when it passes external verifica

NARRATION

The most important thing to understand about coding agents is not what they can do. It is how correctness gets established. Code is not done when it is written. It is not done when it reads correctly. It is done when it passes external verification: tests run, builds complete, type checks pass, linters clear, the diff is reviewed by a human who understands the system. Mark Chen and colleagues (2021) introduced HumanEval, the field's foundational code-evaluation benchmark, precisely because code cannot be judged by appearance.
